prevalence.trend: stable; diagnosis increasing with service awareness

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated suicide and self-harm risk

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: pervasive pattern of instability in relationships, self-image, and affect with impulsivity; diagnosis requires longitudinal assessment

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; best outcomes with structured psychotherapy (DBT) which is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often diagnosed in specialized psychiatry/CAPS; APS identifies risk and refers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Structured psychotherapy (e.g., DBT) as first-line; crisis planning; cautious pharmacotherapy for symptom targets
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited DBT availability, crisis care gaps, and stigma
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS can provide longitudinal follow-up and crisis support where present
